# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Jenkins Pipeline that downloads build artifacts from external URLs provided as parameters and deploys them to production servers. This allows us to deploy pre-built artifacts from different sources (S3, Artifactory, HTTP servers) without rebuilding.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline { 
    agent any   // Specifies that this is an "any" Jenkins node which can run all jobs at once or select a specific one to execute in parallel. Alternatively you could use the name of your custom Node Label, if applicable (if it's not named as 'default') here specified below:
    stages {  // Declare multiple stage blocks for each job/stage we want our Jenkins agent(i)to perform or do within this node when calling out to "build" method. Each block is called an environment and contains a set of steps that the caller wants done in it which would be executed on your defined jenkin's executor as well (like maven, gradle etc.)
        stage('Download Artifact') {  // Stage where we are going to download artifact from S3 bucket. Use 'echo' command for testing purpose only: echo "Getting file ${env}" would print the environment value into console during Jenkins build which can be verified later in log section as well at each step of running this stage
            withCredentials([string(credentialsId : '', usernameVariable : "", passwordVariable : "" , type:'Amazon Web Services')]) {  // Here we are using Amazon's security credentials to download file from S3 bucket. AWS ID and Secret can be configured here or retrieved via IAM in Jenkins
                echo "Getting file ${env}"  
            }   
        }    
         stage('Deploy Artifact') {  // Stage where we are going the deply artifact to production servers by running Maven/Gradle command. You would use 'sh' for bash commands and similar constructs can be used depending on your system setup ie Unix or Windows Shell Command Executor, it should point out location of mvn (for gradle)
            sh "mvn clean install"  // Run Maven/Gradle command to build the project. You would use appropriate commands based upon configuration details provided in Jenkinsfile and dependencies declared inside pom file or equivalent for respective tool(MATLAB, NEXUS etc.)   which depends on your tools used
        }   
     }  //End of Stages block declaration part        
}// Ends Pipeline Blocks. Your pipeline will run all the jobs under each environment (stage) and based upon results it can be further controlled by Jenkins dashboard or via CLI(Command Line Interface). Also note, to see these logs during builds you need a 'Logging Plugin' in your jenkin instance installed
